name: Shellfish Gazpacho
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat 2 tablespoons Olive oil in a skillet over medium heat. Add shrimp and scallops. Cover and cook until shrimp and scallops are cooked, about 4 minutes. Remove shrimp and scallops and place them in the refrigerator to chill.
2. Add clams, mussels, white wine, vinegar, onion, and garlic to the pan. Cover. Increase heat to high and cook until the shells open, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat. Remove shellfish and add them to the shrimp and scallops in the refrigerator to chill.
3. Transfer the contents of the pan to a food processor or blender. Add cucumber, Tabasco, garlic, and 1 cup tomato juice. Blend until smooth. Add mayonnaise, blend until incorporated, and taste for salt and pepper. Pour the mixture into a bowl and place it in the refrigerator to chill.
4. Roast the peppers, remove the skin and seeds, and place them in the refrigerator to chill. Brush rounds of bread with remaining olive oil. Toast under a broiler and set aside to cool.
5. When ready for dinner, arrange 2 shrimp and 2 scallops in each pepper half. Spoon some gazpacho around the peppers and arrange clams and mussels in the soup. Place croutons on top of the peppers. Serve well-chilled.
